content:
ASHITABA: THE SECRET OF JAPANESE LONGEVITY

What is Ashitaba?

Ashitaba is a native Japanese apiaceous plant that has its origin on Hachijo Island, Japan. The inhabitants of Hachijo Island are well known for their longevity and good health. Ashitaba has been an integral part of their diet for hundreds of years. It has been taken as health food and also utilized as medical herb.

Ashitaba is a potent food for vitality and energy. Due to its rich nutrition and remarkable healing effects, Ashitaba gained its fine reputation as longevity grass.

Ashitaba means "Tomorrow Leaf" in Japanese. Its vital life force is so strong that it is believed to grow a new leaf tomorrow is you were to pick one of its leaves today.

Nutritional Values of Ashitaba

Ashitaba is an ideal food for vegans and vegetarians because it has a broad range of nutrients. It contains 11 different types of vitamins, 13 types of minerals, phytochemicals, chlorophyll, fibre, beta-carotene, enzymes, etc.

Nutrition Facts Per Serving 100g:
• Protein: 17.3g
• Fat: 6.3g
• Carbohydrate: 20g
• Dietary Fiber: 44.5g
• Energy: 294kcal
• Sodium: 154mg
• Phosphorus: 321mg
• Iron: 11.5mg
• Calcium: 1.58g
• Potassium: 2.84g
• Magnesium: 103mg
• Vitamin A: 1140mcg
• Vitamin B1: 390mcg
• Vitamin B2: 820mcg
• Vitamin B6: 540mcg
• Vitamin C: 37mg
• Vitamin E: 22mg
• Vitamin K1: 2620mcg
• Niacin: 5.67mg
• Lutein: 33.2mg
• Pantothenic acid: 1.26mg
• Chlorophyll: 384mg

Vitamin B12

Most plants are completely lacking in vitamin B12, which is normally only obtainable through meat, fish and eggs. However, Ashitaba is a good source of this important nutrient, making it an ideal supplement for strict vegans and vegetarians, who omit these foods from their diets and are at risk of suffering from a deficiency. A shortage of vitamin B12 can cause serious cognitive and nervous system problems, in addition to increasing the risk of cardiovascular disease and pernicious anaemia.

Therapeutic Compounds in Ashitaba

Researchers believe that Ashitaba is effective against many medical problems as a result of the synergistic action of its various active compounds. In addition to the vitamins and minerals it contains, Ashitaba also possesses nutrients, which also have specific health-related benefits.

Chalcones

Ashitaba contains a sticky yellow sap, not found in other plants known as chalcone, which are polyphenol compounds unique to Ashitaba. Chalcones in Ashitaba, mainly consisting of Xanthoangelol and 4-hydroxyderricin, has several beneficial bioactive effects, such as an antibacterial effect, an antitumor effect and an effect on lipid metabolism (inhibition of fat accumulation and enhancement of fat burning). Chalcones are potent antioxidants which help to protect our organs from destructive free radicals and slow down aging. Ashitaba out-performs a range of herbs including green tea, dandelion, fennel, black tea, chamomile, Saint Johns Wort and sage for its antioxidant potential.

Germanium

This trace mineral has been found to stimulate the body's production of interferon. Its helps the immune system by boosting the activity of natural killer cells, a type of white blood cell that attacks invading germs. Dr. Otto Warburg, a Nobel Prize winning biochemist, stated that germanium helped to increase the delivery of oxygen to cells. He believed that boosting the oxygen supply to healthy cells slowed the growth of tumours.

Chlorophyll

Ashitaba is rich in chlorophyll, the green pigment in plants that collects strong energy from the sun. The molecule of chlorophyll is almost identical to the hemoglobin molecule in red blood cells and is often referred to as "nature's blood". Besides the ability to stimulate the production of red blood cells, it is also an excellent agent for cleansing the blood and liver and promotes the growth of friendly bacteria in the digestive tract.

Benefits of Ashitaba:
• A super food which is rich in nutrients
• Removes harmful toxic wastes from body
• Removes heavy metal from body
• A potent antioxidant which helps slow down ageing
• Regulate blood sugar
• Strengthens immune system
• Regulate cholesterol level
• Enhance fat burning and inhibit fat accumulation
• Relieves fatigue & tiredness

References:
1. Anti-tumor-promotion by principles obtained from Angelica keiskei. Okuyama T, Planta Med. 1991
2. Antibacterial activity of two chalcones, xanthoangelol and 4-hydroxyderricin, isolated from the root of Angelica keiskei KOIDZUMI. Inamori Y, Chem Pharm Bull (Tokyo). 1991
3. Effects of dietary Angelica keiskei on lipid metabolism in stroke-prone spontaneously hypertensive rats. Ogawa H, Clin Exp Pharmacol Physiol. 2003
4. Angelica keiskei, an emerging medicinal herb with various bioactive constituents and biological activities. Kil YS, Arch Pharm Res. 2017
5. Characterisation, extraction efficiency, stability and antioxidant activity of phytonutrients in Angelica keiskei Lei L, Food Chemistry 115, 2009
